exports.content: Average performance of 12 identically processed vapour-deposited cells is PCE=12.3% +/- 2.0%, Jsc=18.9 +/- 1.8 mA/cm2, Voc=1.05 +/- 0.03 V, FF=0.62 +/- 0.05
exports.label: batch_average_12_3

exports.content: Cl atoms in the mixed-halide perovskite reside in apical positions out of the PbI4 plane rather than in equatorial octahedral sites, consistent with a slight contraction of the c-axis compared to CH3NH3PbI3
exports.label: cl_apical_position

exports.content: The electron and hole diffusion length in CH3NH3PbI3-xClx perovskite is at least 330 nm (the film thickness), as evidenced by efficient charge collection in planar heterojunction cells
exports.label: diffusion_length_lower_bound

exports.content: What are the precise electron and hole diffusion lengths in vapour-deposited CH3NH3PbI3-xClx, and what are the primary excitation and free-charge generation mechanisms?
exports.label: diffusion_length_precise

exports.content: Planar heterojunction p-i-n solar cell with dual-source vapour-deposited CH3NH3PbI3-xClx perovskite: CH3NH3I and PbCl2 evaporated simultaneously at 4:1 molar ratio under 10^-5 mbar onto compact TiO2/FTO substrate, annealed at 100C for 45 min, followed by solution-processed spiro-OMeTAD and evaporated Ag electrode, measured under AM 1.5 at 101 mW/cm2
exports.label: exp_context

exports.content: Will vapour deposition emerge as the preferred route for perovskite solar cell manufacture, or will solution processing eventually match its film quality?
exports.label: manufacturing_route

exports.content: Nanostructuring (mesoporous scaffold) is not necessary to achieve high efficiency with perovskite absorbers; a simple planar heterojunction thin-film architecture can deliver over 15% PCE
exports.label: nanostructure_not_necessary

exports.content: Best vapour-deposited planar heterojunction perovskite cell achieves PCE of 15.4% with Jsc=21.5 mA/cm2, Voc=1.07 V, and fill factor 0.67, measured under AM 1.5 simulated sunlight at 101 mW/cm2
exports.label: pce_15_4_pct

exports.content: Vapour-deposited perovskite films show high phase purity with only minor PbI2 (12.65 degrees) and no measurable CH3NH3PbCl3 (15.68 degrees) XRD peaks, confirming well-formed mixed-halide perovskite
exports.label: phase_purity

exports.content: Both vapour-deposited and solution-cast films produce the same mixed-halide perovskite with orthorhombic crystal structure, as confirmed by identical XRD peak positions at 14.12, 28.44, and 43.23 degrees
exports.label: same_crystal_structure

exports.content: Solution-processed planar heterojunction perovskite cell achieves 8.6% PCE with Jsc=17.6 mA/cm2, Voc=0.84 V, and FF=0.58, but with undulating film (50-410 nm thickness variation) and pinholes causing shunting paths
exports.label: solution_planar_8_6_pct

exports.content: Vapour-deposited perovskite technology is compatible with conventional silicon and CIGS processing, making it suitable as a top cell in hybrid tandem configurations
exports.label: tandem_compatibility

exports.content: Dual-source thermal evaporation: CH3NH3I at ~116C (5.3 A/s) and PbCl2 at ~320C (1 A/s) for ~128 min, producing ~330 nm thick perovskite film after annealing. Substrate holder rotated and water-cooled to ~21C for uniform coating
exports.label: vapour_deposition_method

exports.content: Vapour-deposited perovskite films are extremely uniform with crystalline features on the scale of hundreds of nanometres, in contrast to solution-processed films with partial coverage and crystalline platelets tens of micrometres in size
exports.label: vapour_film_uniformity
